title: JARVIS Step 24 — Panel Glossary
purpose: A plain-language glossary of every visible JARVIS section and key panel: what each means, what healthy / warning / critical readings look like, and what the operator should do. Documentation only — adds no capability, control, or API change.
relation_to_steps_20_23: Step 20 polished the UI; Step 21 proved it live and safe; Step 22 gave the operator runbook; Step 23 set the known-limits and safe roadmap. Step 24 is the reference glossary operators read alongside the runbook.
jarvis_identity: Read-only observer — it mirrors aggregated state and never mutates anything.
No execution authority — no commands, scripts, prompts, or missions are run from the page.
No broker / paper / live control — no order path; paper_ready, live_ready, and broker_control are always false.

panel_id_alias_map.note: The Step 24 spec named four panels with shorthand that are not literal template ids. They are documented here mapped to the real committed ids so nothing is ambiguous.

aliases.note: The 'Documentation & Maps' section, not a single panel.

section_glossary.meaning: The single-glance verdict for the whole system.
The foundational infrastructure: server, AI brains, git repository, project artifacts, brain memory.
Read-only engine status plus the daily plan.
Safety gates, operator posture, and the cached health / route / cache-freshness / hygiene reports.
Read-only trading research detail — no broker, paper, or live.
Tracked, display-only mission board, prompt library, and system wiring map.

panel_glossary.title: Commander's Snapshot
panel_glossary.means: The derived top-level verdict: overall state plus safety, health, route, trading posture, cache, counts, and warnings.
panel_glossary.healthy: overall_state = green (clean posture, all required checks pass).
panel_glossary.warning: overall_state = yellow — most often a dirty/untracked working tree. Needs attention, not approval.
panel_glossary.critical: overall_state = red, or trading posture not research_only.
panel_glossary.operator_action: Read the warnings line. Yellow: note it and continue observing. Red: stop and investigate the failing signal.

panel_glossary.title: System Core
panel_glossary.means: Server running state, brain state, server time, and library counts.
panel_glossary.healthy: Server running; brain online; sensible counts.
panel_glossary.warning: Brain state not online, or counts missing.
panel_glossary.operator_action: If the server isn't running, restart it offline and re-check. Do not add a UI control.

panel_glossary.title: AI Brains
panel_glossary.critical: Brain error / not connected when it is expected.
panel_glossary.operator_action: Warn states are usually informational; investigate offline only if a required brain is down.

panel_glossary.title: Trading Bridge
panel_glossary.means: READ-ONLY / LOCKED trading posture, sealed lifecycle count, and per-lifecycle verdicts.
panel_glossary.healthy: Posture READ-ONLY / LOCKED; lifecycle count as expected.
panel_glossary.critical: Posture shows anything other than read-only / locked.
panel_glossary.operator_action: Critical posture change is a safety regression — stop and investigate. There is no order path here by design.

panel_glossary.title: Health Report
panel_glossary.healthy: Overall pass; py_compile and pytest pass.
panel_glossary.warning: No cached report (manual tool not run) — not a live failure.
panel_glossary.operator_action: If failing, read the stderr tail and fix offline; regenerate the cached report with the manual tool. Never run the tool from the web.

panel_glossary.title: Route Smoke
panel_glossary.means: CACHED GET-only route check with per-route status codes.
panel_glossary.warning: No cached report, or a non-required 404 (e.g. /money-spartan).
panel_glossary.operator_action: Required-route failures: investigate the route offline. Non-required 404s are expected noise.

panel_glossary.title: Cache Freshness
panel_glossary.means: DISPLAY-ONLY cache metadata: per-cache age vs threshold, gitignored status, timestamps. Reads metadata only; never refreshes.
panel_glossary.healthy: Overall fresh; each cache within its threshold.
panel_glossary.warning: stale or missing — a manual cached report is old or not generated (not a live failure).
panel_glossary.critical: invalid / unavailable (corrupt or unreadable cache file).
panel_glossary.operator_action: Stale/missing: regenerate the cached report offline with the manual tool. Invalid: inspect the file offline.

panel_glossary.title: File Hygiene
panel_glossary.means: CACHED git/untracked summary: untracked, tracked-modified, staged counts; top untracked dirs; cached reports stay gitignored.
panel_glossary.warning: Non-zero staged count, or a large untracked backlog.
panel_glossary.critical: A cached report shown as NOT ignored, or git read unavailable.
panel_glossary.operator_action: Investigate a non-zero staged count before commit work; ensure cached reports remain gitignored.

panel_glossary.title: Mission Board
panel_glossary.means: DISPLAY-ONLY tracked operator missions with status/priority. 'Safe next prompt' text is display only.
panel_glossary.warning: NO MISSION BOARD (file missing) or blocked missions present.
panel_glossary.operator_action: Missions are informational. Nothing on this panel executes; never add a run/execute control.

panel_glossary.title: Prompt Library
panel_glossary.means: DISPLAY-ONLY tracked prompts with category / risk / allowed flags. Prompt text is shown, never run.
panel_glossary.operator_action: Read prompts as reference only. There is no copy-and-run affordance, and there must never be one.

panel_glossary.title: System Map
panel_glossary.means: DISPLAY-ONLY wiring map: panels, manual-only scripts, tracked/ignored files, and posture flags.
panel_glossary.healthy: Posture flags: read-only TRUE, browser execution DISABLED, broker control DISABLED, file mutation from web DISABLED.
panel_glossary.critical: Any posture flag flips to the unsafe value (e.g. browser execution ENABLED).
panel_glossary.operator_action: An unsafe posture flag is a serious regression — stop and investigate.

panel_glossary.title: Trading Research Detail
panel_glossary.means: READ-ONLY research detail: candidate status, posture flags, S26 research state, latest report cards.
panel_glossary.healthy: read_only TRUE; paper_ready / live_ready / broker_control all FALSE.
panel_glossary.critical: paper_ready, live_ready, or broker_control reported TRUE.
panel_glossary.operator_action: Any TRUE on paper/live/broker is a CRITICAL safety regression — stop and investigate immediately.

field_glossary.means: The aggregator responded and the page received its payload (dashboard liveness). Not a claim that all subsystems are green.
The whole JARVIS surface is observe-only: no execution, no refresh control, no broker/paper/live path, no web file mutation.

field_glossary.values: green = healthy, yellow = attention needed (often dirty tree), red = stop and investigate.

field_glossary.means: Display-only freshness of the gitignored cached reports (fresh / stale / missing / invalid).
The derived top-level summary object that powers pSnapshot.
The display-only wiring map object that powers pSystemMap, including posture flags.
The read-only research detail object that powers pTradingDetail.
Whether a paper-trading path is armed.
Whether a live-trading path is armed.
Whether JARVIS holds any broker control / order path.

safety_interpretation_rules.critical_note: None of these colours authorize trading or execution.

never_confuse_with_permission: A green dashboard is an observation that the system looks healthy. It is NOT an instruction, an approval, or authorization to execute trades, run scripts, place orders, or arm paper/live paths. JARVIS grants no permissions — it only shows state. Authorization for any action comes from an explicit human decision through the proper, separately-gated channel, never from a colour on this page.
recommended_next_safe_step: JARVIS-STATUS-INTERPRETATION-TABLE-MEMO (Step 25) — a docs-only reference table of every section's possible states and exactly how to read each, complementing this glossary; alternatively JARVIS-REPORT-ARCHIVE-INDEX-MEMO indexing all committed JARVIS step reports.
conclusion: Documentation-only panel glossary created. No code, UI, tool, test, API, or trading surface was changed.
